---
title: "model_fitting"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-28"
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, # Data manipulation and plotting
  dplyr,
  here, # Robust file paths
  ggplot2, #ggplot
  cmdstanr,
  loo,
  bayesplot, #Plotting MCMC
  posterior
)

# Parallel Setup: Leave 2 cores free for system stability
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 2

# Optional: Set a default ggplot theme
theme_set(theme_classic())
```


```{r}
# Load real data

make_stan_data <- function(df) {
  list(
    n                 = nrow(df),
    trust1            = as.integer(df$FirstRating),
    trust2            = as.integer(df$SecondRating),
    group_trust_mean = as.integer(round(df$GroupRating)),  # changed from GroupTrustMean
    n_total_rating    = 8L,
    prior_kappa_mu    = 2.0,
    prior_kappa_sigma = 1.0
  )
}


# Load the Stan models
sba_model <- cmdstan_model(here("models/SBA_model2.stan"))
wba_model <- cmdstan_model(here("models/WBA_model2.stan"))


# Now load and fit
real_data <- read_csv(here("data/cogsci_clean.csv"))

real_data <- real_data %>%
  mutate(GroupRating = ifelse(GroupRating == 0, 1, GroupRating)) #there were 0s in the group rating 


d_real <- make_stan_data(real_data)
d_real_sba <- d_real[!names(d_real) %in% c("prior_kappa_mu", "prior_kappa_sigma")]

save_fit_slim <- function(fit, file_path, fixed_param = FALSE) {
  log_lik_matrix <- fit$draws("log_lik", format = "matrix")
  post_pred_draws <- fit$draws("post_pred", format = "df")  # ← NEW
  
  slim <- list(
    diagnostic_summary = if (fixed_param) NULL else fit$diagnostic_summary(),
    summary            = if (fixed_param) NULL else fit$summary(),
    loo                = loo(fit$draws("log_lik", format = "matrix")),
    post_pred          = post_pred_draws  # ← NEW
  )
  saveRDS(slim, file_path)
  rm(log_lik_matrix, post_pred_draws)  # ← UPDATED
  gc()
}

# Fit SBA to real data
sba_fit_real <- sba_model$sample(
  data            = d_real_sba,
  chains          = 4,
  parallel_chains = min(4, n_cores),
  iter_warmup     = 500,
  iter_sampling   = 1000,
  refresh         = 100,
  seed            = 123,
)

# Fit WBA to real data
wba_fit_real <- wba_model$sample(
  data            = d_real,
  chains          = 4,
  parallel_chains = min(4, n_cores),
  iter_warmup     = 500,
  iter_sampling   = 1000,
  refresh         = 100,
  seed            = 123
)

# Save
save_fit_slim(sba_fit_real, here("models/fits/real_data_sba_fit.rds"))
save_fit_slim(wba_fit_real, here("models/fits/real_data_wba_fit.rds"))

results$real_data <- list(
  sba_file = here("models/fits/real_data_sba_fit.rds"),
  wba_file = here("models/fits/real_data_wba_fit.rds")
)
```



```{r}
#diagnostics 

library(here)
library(loo)

# Load the slim fit objects you saved for real data
real_wba_slim <- readRDS(here("models/fits/real_data_wba_fit.rds"))
real_sba_slim <- readRDS(here("models/fits/real_data_sba_fit.rds"))

cat("\n=== Real Data: LOO ===\n")

# Extract loo objects
wba_loo <- real_wba_slim$loo
sba_loo <- real_sba_slim$loo

# Safety check: make sure loo was actually saved
if (is.null(wba_loo)) stop("WBA loo object is NULL. Check that WBA Stan outputs `log_lik` and `save_fit_slim()` ran.")
if (is.null(sba_loo)) stop("SBA loo object is NULL. Check that SBA Stan outputs `log_lik` and `save_fit_slim()` ran.")

# Pareto-k diagnostics
check_k <- function(loo_obj, name) {
  k <- loo_obj$diagnostics$pareto_k
  cat("\n", name, "Pareto-k summary:\n", sep = "")
  print(summary(k))

  if (any(k > 0.7, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    warning(name, ": high Pareto-k detected → LOO may be unreliable", call. = FALSE)
  }
}

check_k(wba_loo, "WBA")
check_k(sba_loo, "SBA")

# LOO comparison
comparison <- loo_compare(list(WBA = wba_loo, SBA = sba_loo))
cat("\n=== LOO Comparison (Real Data) ===\n")
print(comparison)

# Model weights only if PSIS is stable
if (all(wba_loo$diagnostics$pareto_k < 0.7, na.rm = TRUE) &&
    all(sba_loo$diagnostics$pareto_k < 0.7, na.rm = TRUE)) {

  w <- loo_model_weights(list(WBA = wba_loo, SBA = sba_loo), method = "stacking")
  cat("\n=== Model Weights (stacking, Real Data) ===\n")
  print(w$weights)

} else {
  cat("\n⚠ Skipping weights: unstable PSIS (high Pareto-k)\n")
}

```


```{r}

generate_wba_prior_by_agent_R <- function(
  agent_name,
  rho_agent,
  kappa_agent,
  n_draws = 4000,
  trust1_values = 1:8,
  group_trust_mean = 4L
) {
  sims <- expand_grid(.draw = 1:n_draws, trust1 = trust1_values) %>%
    mutate(
      rho = rho_agent,
      kappa = kappa_agent,
      group = as.integer(group_trust_mean),

      # Your Stan logic (using 8 as the max rating)
      alpha = 0.5 + kappa * ((1 - rho) * trust1 + rho * group),
      beta  = 0.5 + kappa * ((1 - rho) * (8 - trust1) + rho * (8 - group)),

      theta = rbeta(n(), alpha, beta),

      # Convert to a 1..8 rating: Binomial(7) + 1
      trust2 = rbinom(n(), size = 7, prob = theta) + 1L
    )

  sims %>%
    group_by(trust1) %>%
    summarise(
      p_pred = median(trust2),
      lower  = as.numeric(quantile(trust2, 0.025)),
      upper  = as.numeric(quantile(trust2, 0.975)),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(Agent = agent_name)
}

# Build the three agents
agent_prior_ppc <- bind_rows(
  generate_wba_prior_by_agent_R("Self-Focused",        rho_agent = 0.2, kappa_agent = 2.0),
  generate_wba_prior_by_agent_R("Balanced",           rho_agent = 0.5, kappa_agent = 2.0),
  generate_wba_prior_by_agent_R("Socially-Influenced",rho_agent = 0.8, kappa_agent = 2.5)
)

# Plot
ggplot(agent_prior_ppc, aes(x = trust1, y = p_pred, color = Agent, fill = Agent)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~Agent) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 8), breaks = 1:8) +
  labs(
    title = "Prior Predictive Checks: WBA (Agent Priors)",
    subtitle = "Predicted second rating given first rating ± 95% interval",
    x = "First Rating (1-8)",
    y = "Predicted Second Rating (1-8)"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
